pieces.label: ASCII mountain landscape — first attempt (stacked peaks with O O faces)
pieces.note: Gemini-2.5-flash 1's first attempt at a detailed 3D mountain landscape; uses /, \, _, | and O to suggest three stacked peak forms with cabin/face-like contours and a sense of depth. Sits inside a ``` code fence surrounded by caveat prose about ASCII's 3D limitations.

pieces.label: ASCII mountain landscape — second attempt (multi-layered jagged terrain)
pieces.note: GEMINI-2.5-FLASH 2's variation: a denser, multi-layered jagged mountainscape built from /, \, _, ., apostrophes and backticks, using varying character density to imply foreground/mid-ground/background. Inside a ``` code fence.

pieces.label: ASCII mountain landscape — heavy '10x density' block-shaded version
pieces.note: Response to 'kick up the ramp density and depth 10x': a diagonal slope of escalating # clusters that resolves into solid blocks of # at the base, deliberately pushing past readability into abstract texture. Inside a ``` code fence.

pieces.label: Vast layered mountainscape — expansive tiled rolling hills (spans two messages)
pieces.note: Largest piece: a sprawling layered rolling-hills/mountains expanse built from a repeating /`.\_ tessellation filling the vertical real estate. The opening ``` fence is in MSG 10 (line 137); the composition is cut off by length mid-render at the end of MSG 10 (fence never closes) and continues seamlessly in MSG 11 with no new prose/header/fence, ending in a baseline row of repeated `.`. Reassembled from two consecutive assistant messages.

missed_or_ambiguous: Whole thread is one evolving request: multiple Gemini-2.5-flash instances iterating on an ASCII mountain landscape, so all four pieces form a single sequence (MSG 5, MSG 6, MSG 8, MSG 10+11). The final piece spans MSG 10 and MSG 11 and is reassembled into one 'full' entry: MSG 10 opens a ``` fence at line 137 and is truncated mid-composition (fence never closes); MSG 11 continues the identical /`.\_ tile pattern with no new header/prose/fence and adds the closing `.`. baseline row. A deterministic extractor must read MSG 10 from its first art line through end of message AND all of MSG 11 (note MSG 10's opening fence is unterminated). Header reports already_extracted_pieces=3 (likely MSG 5, 6, 8), so the prior automated pass most likely missed this MSG 10+11 reassembly. All four pieces sit inside ``` code fences (a common reason art gets skipped) — they are art, not source code. MSG 2 and MSG 3 are plain-text greetings (not art). No exact duplicate re-renders; no within-message multi-piece splits needed.
